page.content: The demographic data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive overview of variables related to employment conditions and patient contact among the sampled population. The analysis includes critical metrics such as the mean and median values, which offer insights into age distribution and gender representation within the cohort. Notably, the mean age of participants is highlighted, reflecting the general demographic trends in healthcare employment. Additionally, the table delineates the conditions of employment, including the frequency of patient contact among various groups, which is essential for understanding the impact of work environment on health outcomes.

lines.text: The demographic data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive overview of variables related to employment conditions
and patient contact among the sampled population. The analysis includes critical metrics such as the mean and median values,
which offer insights into age distribution and gender representation within the cohort. Notably, the mean age of participants is
highlighted, reflecting the general demographic trends in healthcare employment. Additionally, the table delineates the
conditions of employment, including the frequency of patient contact among various groups, which is essential for
understanding the impact of work environment on health outcomes.

page.html:
DomainDescriptions
1Demographic data: Questions about gender, age, graduation, conditions of employment, and working hours with patient contact [6].
2Working positions when treating patients in carrying out manual work on a daily basis.
3Work-related acute injuries and overuse complaints during the previous year and detailed questions for every body part if these injuries or complaints were currently present [10].
4Psychosocial work environment and questions about inability to work because of mental health problems [14].
5Chronic disease that affects work as a chiropractor in primary care.
6Consequences of work-related physical and/or mental issues [10].

cells.cell_text: Demographic data: Questions about gender, age, graduation, conditions of employment, and working hours with patient contact [6].
Working positions when treating patients in carrying out manual work on a daily basis.
Work-related acute injuries and overuse complaints during the previous year and detailed questions for every body part if these injuries or complaints were currently present [10].
Psychosocial work environment and questions about inability to work because of mental health problems [14].
Chronic disease that affects work as a chiropractor in primary care.
Consequences of work-related physical and/or mental issues [10].

page.content: Furthermore, the standard deviation (SD) values presented in the table indicate the variability within these demographic characteristics, underscoring the diversity in working hours and professional experience among respondents. The inclusion of p-values enhances the statistical rigor of the findings, allowing for interpretation of significance in the observed differences across groups. Collectively, this data sheds light on the relationships between demographic factors and professional practices, serving as a vital resource for further research into the implications of these variables on patient care and outcomes.
